tool.name: Kraken Binarize
tool.command:
ln -s '$image' 'input.${image.ext}' &&
#if $image.ext == 'pdf' kraken #if int($verbose) > 0 #for $i in range(int($verbose)) -v #end for #end if -n --raise-on-error -f pdf -I 'input.${image.ext}' -o .png binarize --threshold '$threshold' --zoom '$zoom' --escale '$escale' --border '$border' --perc '$perc' --range '$range' --low '$low' --high '$high' && echo "PWD: \$(pwd)" >&2 && echo "Files created:" >&2 && find .. -maxdepth 3 -type f -print >&2 #else kraken #if int($verbose) > 0 #for $i in range(int($verbose)) -v #end for #end if -n --raise-on-error --input 'input.${image.ext}' 'kraken_output' binarize --threshold '$threshold' --zoom '$zoom' --escale '$escale' --border '$border' --perc '$perc' --range '$range' --low '$low' --high '$high' && mv kraken_output '$output' #end if

param.name: verbose
param.label: Verbosity level
param.help: Increase logging verbosity. 0=default; higher values add one -v flag per level.

param.label: Ignore this much of the border

tool.help:
**What it does**
Kraken is an OCR and HTR system for historical, non-Latin, and other document images. This wrapper runs only Kraken's binarization step on one document image.
Binarization converts the input image into a binary image suitable for later segmentation and OCR steps.
For image inputs, this tool returns a single PNG image.
For PDF inputs, Kraken is run with PDF batch input mode:
``` kraken -f pdf -I input.pdf -o .png binarize ```
The generated binarized page images are returned as a Galaxy list collection.
**License** Kraken is licensed under the Apache-2.0 license.
